headline: Agent identity and memory architecture dominated the quarter as durable logs and context management moved to the center of coding-agent design.
body: The strongest current this window centered on agent persistence: arguments that coding agents fail from missing context and memory rather than raw capability [0], and that durable session logs, not models or frameworks, define an agent across runtimes [2]. Supporting this, engineering discussion shifted toward building agentic systems through decomposition, separation of concerns, and state management [8], while Google Cloud pushed AI-native databases, hybrid Spanner search, and Model Context Protocol tooling for data agents [6][10][11]. Production evaluation patterns for autonomous systems also surfaced, emphasizing tool use, planning, and drift detection over model accuracy [7]. On the technique side a cluster of new entrants appeared, including feed-forward network, emergent behavior, primitive-action decomposition, and optical flow, while task decomposition led the established movers despite weakening.

cited_items.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
